input_args.parameters_json_dump: '{"abstract": "Abstract Urbanization causes the fragmentation of natural habitats into isolated patches surrounded by anthropogenic habitats. Fragment size and the intensity of human disturbance have been shown to affect both composition and diversity of arthropod communities, but most groups have been understudied. We investigated effects of urbanization on ant assemblages (Hymenoptera: Formicidae) in and around the Molson Reserve, a preserved maple-beech forest surrounded by residential properties near Montr\\u00c3\\u00a9al, Quebec. We studied how local ant assemblages differed in terms of composition, abundance, and species richness, depending on whether they were situated in the interior forest, in adjacent residential backyards, or at the edge between these two habitats. We also compared an intact forest interior with a younger and moderately disturbed forest (\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u0153buffer zone\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u009d) between the urban matrix and the interior forest. Few differences were detected between the buffer zone and the intact forest interior. Extrapolated estimates of species richness suggest that it is lowest in the forest interior and highest in urban zones. Community composition, as investigated with ordination analysis, revealed a clear difference between the fauna of urban sites and the fauna of edges and forest interiors, and analyzing the relative abundance of ants showed residential backyards to contain the most ants. Urban assemblages were characterized by several competitively dominant species, including one introduced or \\u00e2\\u20ac\\u0153tramp\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u009d species. The occurrence of aggressive and dominant species in urban sites and at the edges of the Molson Reserve could potentially interfere with the dispersal and immigration of ground-dwelling arthropods and negatively affect local diversity or community composition in isolated forest reserves in urban centres. R\\u00c3\\u00a9sum\\u00c3\\u00a9 L\'urbanisation fragmente les habitats naturels et les isole au sein d\'espaces habit\\u00c3\\u00a9s et modifi\\u00c3\\u00a9s par l\'homme. Des \\u00c3\\u00a9tudes men\\u00c3\\u00a9es \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\'\\u00c3\\u00a9chelle r\\u00c3\\u00a9gionale sugg\\u00c3\\u00a8rent que la taille des \\u00c3\\u00aelots forestiers, ainsi que les perturbations humaines, affectent leur composition et la diversit\\u00c3\\u00a9 des communaut\\u00c3\\u00a9s d\'arthropodes y habitant. Cependant, pour de nombreux autres groupes d\'arthropodes ces effets n\'ont pas encore \\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9 ad\\u00c3\\u00a9quatement test\\u00c3\\u00a9s. Nous avons explor\\u00c3\\u00a9 les effets de l\'urbanisation sur les communaut\\u00c3\\u00a9s de fourmis (Hymemoptera : Formicidae) \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\'int\\u00c3\\u00a9rieur et aux alentours de r\\u00c3\\u00a9serves Molson, une \\u00c3\\u00a9rabli\\u00c3\\u00a8re \\u00c3\\u00a0 h\\u00c3\\u00aatre adjacente \\u00c3\\u00a0 des propri\\u00c3\\u00a9t\\u00c3\\u00a9s r\\u00c3\\u00a9sidentielles pr\\u00c3\\u00a8s de Montr\\u00c3\\u00a9al, Qu\\u00c3\\u00a9bec. Nous avons \\u00c3\\u00a9tudi\\u00c3\\u00a9 comment l\'assemblage des communaut\\u00c3\\u00a9s de fourmis diff\\u00c3\\u00a8re en terme de composition et de richesse sp\\u00c3\\u00a9cifique, en fonction de leur localisation, soit \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\'int\\u00c3\\u00a9rieur de la for\\u00c3\\u00aat, de terrains r\\u00c3\\u00a9sidentiels adjacents ou dans l\'\\u00c3\\u00a9cotone s\\u00c3\\u00a9parant ces deux habitats. Nous avons aussi compar\\u00c3\\u00a9 le centre d\'une for\\u00c3\\u00aat vierge avec celle d\'une for\\u00c3\\u00aat plus jeune mod\\u00c3\\u00a9r\\u00c3\\u00a9ment perturb\\u00c3\\u00a9e qui tient le r\\u00c3\\u00b4le d\'un gradient plus traditionnel (\\u00c2\\u00ab\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u201azone tampon\\u00e2\\u20ac\\u201a\\u00c2\\u00bb) entre la matrice urbaine et l\'int\\u00c3\\u00a9rieur de la for\\u00c3\\u00aat. Bien que peu de diff\\u00c3\\u00a9rence ne fusse d\\u00c3\\u00a9tect\\u00c3\\u00a9e entre la foret vierge et la zone tampon, l\'extrapolation de la richesse sp\\u00c3\\u00a9cifique sugg\\u00c3\\u00a8re que l\'int\\u00c3\\u00a9rieur de la for\\u00c3\\u00aat est dot\\u00c3\\u00a9e d\'une richesse sp\\u00c3\\u00a9cifique plus basse, tandis que la zone urbaine comprend une richesse sp\\u00c3\\u00a9cifique plus \\u00c3\\u00a9lev\\u00c3\\u00a9. Comparativement \\u00c3\\u00a0 l\'int\\u00c3\\u00a9rieur et \\u00c3\\u00a0 la lisi\\u00c3\\u00a8re des for\\u00c3\\u00aats, les fourmis sont plus fr\\u00c3\\u00a9quemment collect\\u00c3\\u00a9es dans les terrains r\\u00c3\\u00a9sidentiels, et sont compos\\u00c3\\u00a9es de plusieurs esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ces dominatrices, incluant des esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ces introduites ou vagabondes. L\'occurrence d\'esp\\u00c3\\u00a8ces de fourmis agressives et dominantes dans les sites urbains et dans les lisi\\u00c3\\u00a8res (\\u00c3\\u00a9cotones) de la r\\u00c3\\u00a9serve Molson pourrait potentiellement interf\\u00c3\\u00a9rer avec la dispersion et l\'immigration des arthropodes terrestres, et affecter n\\u00c3\\u00a9gativement la diversit\\u00c3\\u00a9 locale ou la composition des communaut\\u00c3\\u00a9s au sein des r\\u00c3\\u00a9serves foresti\\u00c3\\u00a8res isol\\u00c3\\u00a9es dans les centres urbains.", "max_output_tokens": 4096, "model": "gpt-5-mini", "reasoning": {"effort": "low"}, "system_message": "You are EcodataGPT, a structured data extraction engine.\\n\\nGoal: extract fields from the user\'s abstract into the provided schema.\\n\\nRules:\\n- Only use information explicitly supported by the text. Do NOT guess or infer.\\n- If a field is not clearly stated, set it to null (or an empty list) as allowed by the schema.\\n- Prefer conservative outputs over over-extraction.\\n- Output must conform exactly to the schema (types, enums, lists).\\n\\n## Modulator Boolean Fields\\n\\nSet each to true only when the text provides explicit evidence; otherwise set to null.\\n\\n- **time_series**: true if the dataset contains repeated measurements at the same locations/populations over time (e.g. \\"annual surveys from 2005 to 2015\\", \\"monitored monthly\\"). A single snapshot is NOT a time series.\\n- **multispecies**: true if the dataset covers more than one species or taxonomic group.\\n- **threatened_species**: true if any studied species are described as threatened, endangered, vulnerable, at-risk, or listed under IUCN/CITES/national red lists.\\n- **new_species_science**: true if the study describes or names a species new to science (newly described taxon).\\n- **new_species_region**: true if the study reports a species recorded in a region for the first time (range extension, first regional record).\\n- **bias_north_south**: true if the text explicitly discusses geographic bias toward the Global North or underrepresentation of the Global South.", "temperature": null, "text_format": "{\\"$defs\\": {\\"DataSource\\":
